interpreted_controls.control_objective: To ensure that all applicable General requirements relating to 'Regulatory Compliance Obligation', thereby enabling Bank to demonstrate full regulatory adherence and reduce Compliance Risk exposure.
interpreted_controls.control_scope: Applicable entities: Bank, Reserve Bank. Responsible departments: Compliance. Regulatory domain: General.
interpreted_controls.automation_rationale: Obligation fulfilment involves organisational processes and human decisions that cannot be fully automated.
interpreted_controls.business_rationale: Failure to implement 'Regulatory Compliance Obligation' creates uncontrolled Compliance Risk exposure for the institution. Under RBI's supervisory framework, non-compliance with General requirements can result in regulatory censure, financial penalties, and reputational harm. This control operationalises the institution's commitment to regulatory adherence and risk management.
interpreted_controls.control_summary: [MEDIUM] Regulatory Compliance Obligation — Compliance Control is a operational control supporting the 'Regulatory Compliance Obligation' business capability. Implementation follows the 'Workflow Creation' pattern and is owned by the Compliance function. Compliance is verified through manual audit.

interpreted_controls.control_objective: To establish authorised procedures for all applicable Foreign Exchange requirements relating to 'Anti-Money Laundering Governance', thereby enabling the institution to demonstrate full regulatory adherence and reduce Financial Risk exposure.
interpreted_controls.control_scope: Responsible departments: Compliance, Treasury, Risk. Regulatory domain: Foreign Exchange, AML.
interpreted_controls.automation_rationale: This control type relies primarily on human judgment and process adherence, limiting automation applicability.
interpreted_controls.business_rationale: Anti-money laundering failures result in some of the largest regulatory fines globally. 'Anti-Money Laundering Governance' ensures that suspicious activity is detected, reported, and investigated in a timely manner, protecting the institution from complicity in financial crime and from regulatory enforcement action.
interpreted_controls.control_summary: [LOW] Foreign Exchange Authorisation Control is a governance control supporting the 'Anti-Money Laundering Governance' business capability. Implementation follows the 'Workflow Creation' pattern and is owned by the Compliance function. Compliance is verified through manual audit.

interpreted_controls.control_objective: To ensure that all applicable Foreign Exchange requirements relating to 'FEMA Compliance Control', thereby enabling Bank to demonstrate full regulatory adherence and reduce Financial Risk exposure.
interpreted_controls.control_scope: Applicable entities: Bank, Reserve Bank. Responsible departments: Risk, Treasury. Regulatory domain: Foreign Exchange.
interpreted_controls.automation_rationale: Obligation fulfilment involves organisational processes and human decisions that cannot be fully automated.
interpreted_controls.business_rationale: FEMA violations attract compounding penalties and adverse regulatory attention. 'FEMA Compliance Control' ensures that all foreign exchange transactions are authorised, documented, and reported in accordance with RBI Master Directions, protecting the institution from financial and legal liability.
interpreted_controls.control_summary: [MEDIUM] Foreign Exchange Compliance Control is a operational control supporting the 'FEMA Compliance Control' business capability. interpreted_controls.control_objective: To promote adoption of all applicable KYC requirements relating to 'Know Your Customer (KYC) Compliance', thereby enabling banks to demonstrate full regulatory adherence and reduce Compliance Risk exposure.
interpreted_controls.control_scope: Applicable entities: Bank, Reserve Bank, AD Category-I, Customer. Responsible departments: Compliance, Board. Regulatory domain: KYC, AML.
interpreted_controls.automation_rationale: Policy controls require human judgment to evaluate completeness, appropriateness, and board-level approval status. Existence checks can be automated.
interpreted_controls.business_rationale: Inadequate customer due diligence exposes the institution to exploitation by financial criminals. 'Know Your Customer (KYC) Compliance' ensures that customer identities are verified and risk-profiled before account opening, protecting the institution from being used as a conduit for money laundering, terrorist financing, or fraud — all of which carry severe regulatory and reputational consequences.
interpreted_controls.control_summary: [LOW] Kyc Advisory Control is a policy control supporting the 'Know Your Customer (KYC) Compliance' business capability. Implementation follows the 'Policy Update' pattern and is owned by the Compliance function. Compliance is verified through policy review.

interpreted_controls.automation_rationale: Audit quality assessment requires professional judgment. Automated pre-checks can verify audit coverage but cannot replace auditor opinion.
interpreted_controls.business_rationale: Failure to implement 'Digital Payments Governance' creates uncontrolled Operational Risk exposure for the institution. Under RBI's supervisory framework, non-compliance with Audit requirements can result in regulatory censure, financial penalties, and reputational harm. This control operationalises the institution's commitment to regulatory adherence and risk management.
interpreted_controls.control_summary: [LOW] Audit Advisory Control is a governance control supporting the 'Digital Payments Governance' business capability. Implementation follows the 'Evidence Collection' pattern and is owned by the Risk function. Compliance is verified through manual audit.

interpreted_controls.control_objective: To promote adoption of all applicable Foreign Exchange requirements relating to 'Anti-Money Laundering Governance', thereby enabling banks to demonstrate full regulatory adherence and reduce Financial Risk exposure.
interpreted_controls.control_scope: Applicable entities: AD Category-I. Responsible departments: Compliance, Treasury, Risk. Regulatory domain: Foreign Exchange, AML.
interpreted_controls.automation_rationale: This control type relies primarily on human judgment and process adherence, limiting automation applicability.
interpreted_controls.business_rationale: Anti-money laundering failures result in some of the largest regulatory fines globally. 'Anti-Money Laundering Governance' ensures that suspicious activity is detected, reported, and investigated in a timely manner, protecting the institution from complicity in financial crime and from regulatory enforcement action.
interpreted_controls.control_summary: [LOW] Foreign Exchange Advisory Control is a advisory control supporting the 'Anti-Money Laundering Governance' business capability. Implementation follows the 'Workflow Creation' pattern and is owned by the Compliance function. Compliance is verified through manual audit.

interpreted_controls.control_objective: To promote adoption of all applicable Audit requirements relating to 'Know Your Customer (KYC) Compliance', thereby enabling banks to demonstrate full regulatory adherence and reduce Compliance Risk exposure.
interpreted_controls.control_scope: Applicable entities: AD Category-I. Responsible departments: Compliance, Internal Audit. Regulatory domain: Audit, KYC.
interpreted_controls.automation_rationale: Report generation and submission can be automated, but regulatory data quality validation requires human review.
interpreted_controls.business_rationale: Inadequate customer due diligence exposes the institution to exploitation by financial criminals. 'Know Your Customer (KYC) Compliance' ensures that customer identities are verified and risk-profiled before account opening, protecting the institution from being used as a conduit for money laundering, terrorist financing, or fraud — all of which carry severe regulatory and reputational consequences.
interpreted_controls.control_summary: [LOW] Audit Advisory Control is a reporting control supporting the 'Know Your Customer (KYC) Compliance' business capability. Implementation follows the 'Periodic Review' pattern and is owned by the Compliance function. Compliance is verified through evidence upload.

interpreted_controls.control_scope: Applicable entities: Bank, Reserve Bank, AD Category-I. Responsible departments: Compliance. Regulatory domain: General.
interpreted_controls.automation_rationale: This control type relies primarily on human judgment and process adherence, limiting automation applicability.
interpreted_controls.business_rationale: Failure to implement 'Regulatory Best Practice Advisory' creates uncontrolled Compliance Risk exposure for the institution. Under RBI's supervisory framework, non-compliance with General requirements can result in regulatory censure, financial penalties, and reputational harm. This control operationalises the institution's commitment to regulatory adherence and risk management.
interpreted_controls.control_summary: [LOW] Compliance Advisory Control is a advisory control supporting the 'Regulatory Best Practice Advisory' business capability. Implementation follows the 'Workflow Creation' pattern and is owned by the Compliance function. Compliance is verified through manual audit.

interpreted_controls.control_objective: To ensure that all applicable General requirements relating to 'Regulatory Compliance Obligation', thereby enabling banks to demonstrate full regulatory adherence and reduce Compliance Risk exposure.
interpreted_controls.control_scope: Applicable entities: AD Category-I. Responsible departments: Compliance, Operations. Regulatory domain: General.
interpreted_controls.automation_rationale: Obligation fulfilment involves organisational processes and human decisions that cannot be fully automated.
interpreted_controls.business_rationale: Failure to implement 'Regulatory Compliance Obligation' creates uncontrolled Compliance Risk exposure for the institution.
